subtasks.description: Query CloudWatch metrics and alarms to identify the affected service(s), including alarm name, metric threshold breaches, and timestamp of incident onset
Retrieve relevant error and access logs from S3 for the identified service, filtering by incident time window and error severity
Create a new Slack incident channel with naming convention incident-<service>-<timestamp>, set topic, and configure notification settings

Analyze CloudWatch metrics, logs, and service dependencies to determine incident severity (critical/high/medium/low), affected user count, and service impact scope. Generate initial severity assessment summary
Post initial incident summary to Slack channel including severity level, affected service, impact scope, incident start time, and initial remediation steps

plan.cost_rationale: This incident response workflow requires 5 subtasks to handle the multi-tool orchestration needed for enterprise incident management. Subtask 1 (cloudwatch_identify, mid tier) uses multitool_plan to query CloudWatch and retrieve alarm state—mid tier to handle multiple metric queries and context. Subtask 2 (s3_fetch_logs, mid tier) depends on incident identification and uses multitool_plan to fetch and filter logs by service and time window. Subtask 3 (slack_channel_create, small tier) is a simple channel creation call independent of log fetching, assigned small tier. Subtask 4 (severity_assessment, mid tier) uses cross_recon to synthesize signals from CloudWatch and S3 logs and produce a severity classification—mid tier because it reconciles two data sources and requires judgment. Subtask 5 (post_slack_summary, small tier) is a straightforward message post using multitool_plan, assigned small tier. The plan respects dependencies: CloudWatch runs first, then logs and channel creation can happen in parallel, then severity is assessed, and finally the summary is posted. No policy_action is added because incident severity assessment is contextual analysis, not a governance compliance check.

arguments.message:
:rotating_light: *INCIDENT SUMMARY*

*Severity Level:* $2.alerts[0].severity
*Affected Service:* $2.alerts[0].service_tags
*Impact Scope:* $upstream.severity_assessment.service_impact_scope
*Incident Start Time:* $2.alerts[0].triggered_at
*Alarm:* $2.alerts[0].alarm_name
*Metric:* $2.alerts[0].metric_name (Current: $2.alerts[0].current_value | Threshold: $2.alerts[0].threshold)

*Initial Remediation Steps:*
:one: Monitor error rates and system metrics in real-time
:two: Review recent deployment changes and rollback if necessary
:three: Scale resources if capacity-related
:four: Engage service owner and on-call engineering team
:five: Document incident timeline and root cause analysis

*Next Actions:*
• Continuous monitoring active
• Log analysis in progress
• Status updates in thread
